attention3.ptxt: There are two types of OBD system depending on the vehicle specification. For identification, refer to Precaution for OBD System .

servinfosub.title: Euro-OBD Model
servinfosub.ptxt: TCM in this vehicle has the following functions.

item.ptxt: When ignition “ON” with the engine at a stop, MIL (1) turns ON in order to check the MIL and its circuit.

When TCM detects a malfunction in A/T control system, it makes MIL in the combination meter turn ON and stores the Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) in its memory. However, if it detects that the system has been normal for continuously 3 driving cycles after detecting a malfunction, it will make MIL turn OFF, although DTC stored in TCM memory will remain as a history DTC.

It is possible to communicate with TCM through Data Link Connector (DLC) (2) by using not only SUZUKI scan tool but also CAN communication OBD generic scan tool. (Diagnostic information can be checked and erased by using scan tool.)
For information about the following items, refer to OBD System Description .
When ignition is “ON” with no malfunction in A/T control system detected, transmission warning light (1) lights for about 2 seconds and then goes OFF for bulb check.
When TCM detects a malfunction in A/T control system, it indicates transmission warning light and stores DTC in its memory.
It is possible to communicate with TCM through DLC (2) by using SUZUKI scan tool. Diagnostic information can be checked and erased by using SUZUKI scan tool.
For information about the following items, refer to OBD System Description .
